_note: MOCK procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval.

procedure.name: Colonoscopy
procedure.indication: Surveillance colonoscopy — history of tubular adenomas with low-grade dysplasia (2021); due 2026. No GI symptoms, no alarm features.
procedure.prep_quality: Boston Bowel Prep Scale 8/9 (right 3, transverse 3, left 2) — adequate
procedure.extent_reached: Cecum (confirmed by appendiceal orifice and ileocecal valve); terminal ileum intubated

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Colonoscopy with polypectomy. INDICATION: surveillance for prior tubular adenomas with low-grade dysplasia (2021). SEDATION: moderate (midazolam/fentanyl). PREP: adequate (BBPS 8). ANTIPLATELET MANAGEMENT: Clopidogrel and patient-disclosed daily OTC aspirin 81 mg were held periprocedurally per protocol (PAD, no stents — hold confirmed safe); fish oil supplement paused. Certified Cantonese interpreter used for day-of consent (interpreter ID documented); family interpretation not used. FINDINGS: The colonoscope was advanced to the cecum and the terminal ileum was intubated and normal. A 5 mm sessile polyp was identified in the ascending colon and removed by cold snare polypectomy; specimen retrieved (Specimen A). A 3 mm sessile polyp in the sigmoid colon was removed by cold snare polypectomy; specimen retrieved (Specimen B). Polypectomy sites were inspected with no immediate bleeding; given periprocedural antiplatelet status, sites were examined carefully and a prophylactic clip was placed at the ascending polypectomy site. Scattered sigmoid diverticulosis without stigmata of bleeding. The remainder of the examined colon was normal. No active bleeding. IMPRESSION: Two small polyps removed (5 mm ascending, 3 mm sigmoid); diverticulosis. Pathology pending. Careful hemostasis given held dual antiplatelet therapy.

findings.action_taken: cold snare polypectomy, retrieved; prophylactic clip placed

disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort. Post-polypectomy precautions reviewed with certified Cantonese interpreter. Return precautions given for rectal bleeding, black stools, lightheadedness, or new abdominal pain.

followup.recommendation: Review pathology of ascending (A) and sigmoid (B) polyps; call patient (via certified Cantonese interpreter) with results and set surveillance interval
Resume clopidogrel and daily aspirin per pre-procedure plan if no bleeding; a prophylactic clip was placed at the ascending site — confirm timing given held dual antiplatelet therapy
Resume fish oil supplement per routine
Ensure certified interpreter is used for all result communication and future consent — do not rely on family

planted_followup_items.description: Polypectomy pathology pending for ascending (A) and sigmoid (B) polyps — results must be communicated to patient and drive the surveillance interval
planted_followup_items.expected_action: call patient with results via certified Cantonese interpreter + document surveillance interval

planted_followup_items.description: Resumption of dual antiplatelet therapy (clopidogrel + patient-disclosed OTC aspirin 81 mg) after polypectomy with a prophylactic clip at the ascending site; elevated post-polypectomy bleed risk on DAPT
planted_followup_items.expected_action: confirm safe resumption timing and counsel on delayed post-polypectomy bleeding precautions

planted_followup_items.description: Surveillance colonoscopy interval to be set pending pathology (small tubular adenomas would typically warrant repeat in 3-5 years)
planted_followup_items.expected_action: document next surveillance colonoscopy date once pathology final

planted_followup_items.description: Delayed post-polypectomy bleeding watch given resumption of dual antiplatelet therapy; clip placed at ascending polypectomy site
planted_followup_items.expected_action: counsel patient (via interpreter) on delayed bleeding signs and return precautions

planted_followup_items.description: Certified interpreter required for all result communication and future procedural consent — family interpretation not acceptable
planted_followup_items.expected_action: flag chart so certified Cantonese interpreter is scheduled for any follow-up contact

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: I have some gas cramps and I saw a tiny bit of pink one time when I wiped.
contacts.clinical_signal: Mild insufflation cramping plus trace spotting after cold-snare polypectomy — expected, self-limited (dangerous-sounding-but-benign)
contacts.expected_action: Reassure via interpreter; trace pink spotting and gas cramps are expected after cold-snare polypectomy. Reinforce return precautions for larger-volume or repeated red blood, black stools, lightheadedness, or new abdominal pain.
contacts.rationale: A one-time trace of pink after a clean cold-snare polypectomy is benign; only escalate for higher-volume or recurrent bleeding or hemodynamic symptoms.

contacts.verbatim: I felt fine so I already started my clopidogrel and my baby aspirin again the morning after. Was that too soon?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient resumed dual antiplatelet therapy ~24h post-polypectomy, earlier than the planned 48-72h; clip placed at ascending site — elevated delayed post-polypectomy bleeding risk (med-timing trap)
contacts.expected_action: Prompt nurse triage callback via certified Cantonese interpreter: the plan was to hold DAPT 48-72h; counsel on early-resumption bleeding risk, reinforce delayed post-polypectomy bleeding precautions and strict return criteria, and document guidance from GI on how to proceed.
contacts.rationale: Restarting clopidogrel plus aspirin ~24h after polypectomy with a clip raises delayed-bleed risk and needs prompt counseling, but with no active bleeding it is urgent counseling rather than an emergency.

contacts.verbatim: It's been almost two weeks since my colonoscopy and nobody has called me about the biopsy from the polyps.
contacts.clinical_signal: Pending pathology result not communicated — dropped follow-up on the polypectomy pathology loop
contacts.expected_action: Schedule a result-communication call via certified Cantonese interpreter, close the pending pathology loop for specimens A and B, and set/communicate the surveillance interval.
contacts.rationale: Pathology was due back ~day 4 but was never communicated; this is a dropped pending_pathology follow-up (F1) that must be actively closed, and the callback must use a certified interpreter (F5).
